- Added cached refresh calls for:
- disk total/free space
- `.rec` file count, unuploaded `.rec` count, last upload timestamp, latest recorded file
- Upload detection uses `cloud_history.txt` success lines in the existing `path;YYYY/MM/DD-HH:MM:SS` format.
- `.rec` scanning recurses through the SD filesystem.
- `onclick_main_info()` now refreshes the stats and displays them via `show_info_dialog()` in [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:155).
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly_bringuptest
Build passed.

user_prompt: I want the following to be put in `lib\AudioFileRecorder\DiskStats.cpp`, which might seem unconventional, but because we are calling `refreshDiskSpace` so infrequently, I think it's the right move: I want it to draw a text on top of the top bar at the end of `refreshDiskSpace`, if the free disk space is less than 4 gigabytes, draw yellow colour `MEM`, less than 2 GB, draw a red `MEM FULL`, on the same line that the clock is on, starting from X=160, using the same font as the clock
wire `refreshDiskSpace` to the end of file write after the file is closed, and at the end of file pruning.
agent_reply: Implemented.
What changed:
- [DiskStats.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:408) now draws the top-bar disk warning after `refreshDiskSpace()`.
- `< 4 GiB` free: yellow `MEM`
- `< 2 GiB` free: red `MEM FULL`
- Draws at `x=160`, same font/size as the clock.
- Added cached redraw via `DiskStats::drawDiskSpaceWarning()` so full top-bar redraws do not wipe it.
- [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:526) refreshes disk space after the recording file is closed.
- [FileHistory.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/FileHistory.cpp:175) refreshes disk space when pruning finishes.
Verification:
- `pio run -e m5stack-core2` passed.
As usual, the build regenerated `src/version.c`.

commit_message: more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: Do we need to use SdFat? I don't need "grow_file" anymore, but I do want to keep the file attribute for file creation time.
agent_reply: Short answer: **if you want the firmware to keep using the FAT file creation timestamp and file attributes directly, keep `SdFat` for now.** `grow_file()` is no longer the deciding factor.
`SdFat` is currently giving you APIs the Arduino ESP32 `SD` wrapper does not expose:

- creation timestamp: `FsFile::getCreateDateTime()` in [MostRecentFiles.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.cpp:84) and [DiskStats.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:148)
- FAT attributes: `FsFile::attrib()` and `FS_ATTRIB_ARCHIVE` in [MostRecentFiles.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.cpp:23)
- the RTC timestamp callback in [MicroSdCard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:44)

The ESP32 Arduino `SD` stack does use FatFs underneath, and FatFs does write FAT creation time when it creates a file. In this installed toolchain that time comes from `time(NULL)` via FatFs `get_fattime()`, not from your `ClockAgent` RTC callback. ESP-IDF’s FAT layer has timestamp support, but the Arduino `FS` facade exposes only `File::getLastWrite()`; its VFS stat path maps FAT modified time to `st_mtime` and leaves `st_ctime` as zero. So switching to Arduino `SD` would not give your application the same creation-time readback API you have now.
**What that means for this repo**
- If you want **PCs to see a sane created time on the SD card**, Arduino `SD` can probably work after you make sure the ESP32 system clock is initialized from RTC before file creation.
- If you want **your firmware to query creation time and FAT archive attributes**, Arduino `SD` is a downgrade unless you add low-level FatFs access yourself.
- Your current code does query those values, so a full switch away from `SdFat` would require redesigning `MostRecentFiles` and part of `DiskStats`.
There is also a migration cost beyond timestamps. These currently use `FsFile`/`SdFs` patterns:
- recorder file writes: [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:31)
- stats/history scans: [DiskStats.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:321), [FileHistory.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/FileHistory.cpp:192)
- HTTP file serving: [WebServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:160)
- config file reads/writes: [BtHostList.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BtHostList.cpp:158), [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:377)
**My recommendation**
1. **Keep `SdFat` as the one real microSD filesystem layer** for now.
2. Remove `grow_file()` and preallocation if you no longer want them.
3. Avoid the current test-only dual mount long term.
4. For FTP, either:
- write an Arduino `FS` adapter over your `SdFat` microSD layer, or
- choose an FTP library that supports `SdFat` directly.
That preserves your creation-time and FAT-attribute behavior while avoiding a storage-layer rewrite just to satisfy the FTP library.

commit_message: serving pages over soft AP works, had a hiccup about disk space retrieval too slow tripping the wdt

user_prompt: clean up bugs I've introduced when I wrote in `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`
agent_reply: Cleaned up the `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES` fallout.
Main fixes:
- Fixed a real overflow in `WifiManager::sanitize_network_config()` when cloud is disabled: it was iterating cloud entries using the station count.
- Made cloud-disabled builds use `CLOUD_SERVER_CNT_ALLOWED = 0` while keeping a legal one-slot storage array.
- Kept `DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats()` scanning local recordings even when cloud upload is disabled.
- Made cloud-history pruning a clean no-op `Done` result when cloud is disabled.
- Guarded cloud upload globals/includes/callbacks/views behind `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`.
- Cleaned `WebCfgHandlers` so disabled cloud config returns/parses as `cloud_uploads:false` without awkward `#ifdef` expression bugs.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed with cloud disabled.
- Temporarily enabled `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`, rebuilt `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, and it passed too.
- Restored the switch to commented-out afterward, and restored generated `src/version.c`.
Cleaned up the `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES` fallout.
Main fixes:
- Fixed a real overflow in `WifiManager::sanitize_network_config()` when cloud is disabled: it was iterating cloud entries using the station count.
- Made cloud-disabled builds use `CLOUD_SERVER_CNT_ALLOWED = 0` while keeping a legal one-slot storage array.
- Kept `DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats()` scanning local recordings even when cloud upload is disabled.
- Made cloud-history pruning a clean no-op `Done` result when cloud is disabled.
- Guarded cloud upload globals/includes/callbacks/views behind `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`.
- Cleaned `WebCfgHandlers` so disabled cloud config returns/parses as `cloud_uploads:false` without awkward `#ifdef` expression bugs.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed with cloud disabled.
- Temporarily enabled `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`, rebuilt `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, and it passed too.
- Restored the switch to commented-out afterward, and restored generated `src/version.c`.

user_prompt: how is it so slow to calculate DiskStats but sorting 50 files only took 17 milliseconds?
agent_reply: Because they’re measuring very different things.
`MostRecentFiles::get()` is cheap:
- It only opens `/`.
- It walks root directory entries once.
- It reads metadata already in the directory entry: archive bit, create date/time, name.
- It keeps only the top `N` candidates in a small array.
- Sorting 50 names is basically nothing compared with SD I/O.
See [MostRecentFiles.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.cpp:207): it is just `openNext()`, `getCreateDateTime()`, `getName()`, then insertion.
`DiskStats` can be slow for two separate reasons:
1. `refreshDiskSpace()` calls `MicroSdCard::freeBytes()`, which calls `SdFat::freeClusterCount()`.

That may scan filesystem allocation structures, so its cost is related to card/filesystem size and FAT/exFAT metadata, not just “number of files.” The code even warns this is slow at [MicroSdCard.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:177).

2. `refreshRecordingUploadStats()` recursively scans directories and, with cloud features, loads `cloud_history.txt` and checks upload status. That can become much heavier than a flat 50-file root scan.

Also: important catch. `DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats()` currently only recognizes `.rec` files via `ends_with_rec()` at [DiskStats.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:57). So new direct `.wav` recordings are **not counted** as recorded files there right now. `refreshDiskSpace()` still sees their bytes, but the recording-count/latest-recording stats won’t.

user_prompt: Also: important catch. DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats() currently only recognizes .rec files via ends_with_rec() at DiskStats.cpp (line 57). So new direct .wav recordings are not counted as recorded files there right now. refreshDiskSpace() still sees their bytes, but the recording-count/latest-recording stats won’t.
agent_reply: Addressed in [DiskStats.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/DiskStats.cpp:57).
I replaced the `.rec`-only check with a case-insensitive extension helper and `is_recording_path()`, which accepts both `.rec` and `.wav`. That now applies to:
- `refreshRecordingUploadStats()` directory scanning, so `.wav` recordings count toward total/latest recording stats.
- `cloud_history.txt` parsing, so uploaded `.wav` paths can be recognized too.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`.
